children.name: parent_sample_id
children.description: The unique identifier from HuBMAP or SenNet for the sample (such as a block, section, or suspension) used to perform the assay. For instance, in an RNAseq assay, the parent sample would be the suspension, while in imaging assays, it would be the tissue section. If the assay is derived from multiple parent samples, this field should contain a comma-separated list of identifiers. Example: HBM386.ZGKG.235, HBM672.MKPK.442
children.label: Parent sample ID

children.name: lab_id
children.description: A locally assigned identifier provided by the data provider for the dataset. It is used to reference an external metadata record that may be maintained independently, enabling traceability and supporting provenance tracking. Example: Visium_9OLC_A4_S1
children.label: Lab ID

children.name: preparation_protocol_doi
children.description: The DOI for the protocols.io page that details the assay or the procedures used for sample procurement and preparation. For example, in the case of an imaging assay, the protocol may start with tissue section staining and end with the generation of an OME-TIFF file. The documented protocol should also include any image processing steps involved in producing the final OME-TIFF. Example: https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.eq2lyno9qvx9/v1
children.label: Preparation protocol DOI

children.name: dataset_type
children.description: The specific type of dataset being produced. Example: RNAseq
children.label: Dataset type

children.name: analyte_class
children.description: The analyte class which is the target molecule that the assay is measuring. Example: DNA
children.label: Analyte class

children.name: is_targeted
children.description: Indicates whether a specific molecule or set of molecules is targeted for detection or measurement by the assay. Example: Yes
children.label: Is targeted?

children.name: acquisition_instrument_vendor
children.description: The company that manufactures or supplies the acquisition instrument. An acquisition instrument is a device equipped with signal detection hardware and signal processing software. It captures signals produced by assays, such as variations in light intensity or color, or signals corresponding to molecular mass. If the instrument was custom-built or developed internally, enter "In-House". Example: Illumina
children.label: Acquisition instrument vendor

children.name: acquisition_instrument_model
children.description: The specific model of the acquisition instrument, as manufacturers often offer various versions with differing features or sensitivities. These differences may be relevant to the processing or interpretation of the data. If the instrument was custom-built or developed internally, enter "In-House". If the model is unknown, enter "Unknown". Example: HiSeq 4000
children.label: Acquisition instrument model

children.name: source_storage_duration_value
children.description: The length of time the sample was stored prior to processing it. For assays performed on tissue sections, this refers to how long the tissue section (e.g., slide) was stored before the assay began (e.g., imaging). For assays performed on suspensions, such as sequencing, it refers to how long the suspension was stored before library construction started. Example: 12
children.label: Source storage duration value

children.name: source_storage_duration_unit
children.description: The unit of measurement used to specify the source storage duration value. Example: hour
children.label: Source storage duration unit

children.name: time_since_acquisition_instrument_calibration_value
children.description: The length of time since the acquisition instrument was last serviced or calibrated. This provides a metric for assessing drift in data capture. Example: 10
children.label: Time since acquisition instrument calibration value

children.name: time_since_acquisition_instrument_calibration_unit
children.description: The unit of measurement used to specify the time since acquisition instrument calibration value. Example: month
children.label: Time since acquisition instrument calibration unit

default_value.label: Time since acquisition instrument calibration unit

children.name: contributors_path
children.description: The name of the file containing the ORCID IDs for all contributors to this dataset. Example: ./contributors.csv
children.label: Contributors path

children.name: data_path
children.description: The top-level directory containing the raw and/or processed data. For a single dataset upload, this might be represented as ".", whereas for a data upload containing multiple datasets, this would be the directory name for the respective dataset. For example, if the data is within a directory named "TEST001-RK", use the syntax "./TEST001-RK" for this field. If there are multiple directory levels, use the format "./TEST001-RK/Run1/Pass2", where "Pass2" is the subdirectory where the single dataset's data is stored. This is an internal metadata field used solely for data ingestion. Example: ./TEST001-RK
children.label: Data path

children.name: ms_ionization_technique
children.description: The ionization technique used in imaging mass spectrometry, which refers to the method employed to probe the sample. Example: MALDI
children.label: MS ionization technique

children.name: ms_scan_mode
children.description: The mode of mass spectrometry (MS) scanning, which refers to the number of steps involved in the separation of fragments during the analysis. Example: MS1
children.label: MS scan mode

children.name: mass_analysis_polarity
children.description: The polarity mode used in mass analysis, indicating whether positive or negative ion modes are employed. Example: Positive ion mode
children.label: Mass analysis polarity

children.name: mass_to_charge_range_low_value
children.description: The low value of the scanned mass-to-charge range for MS1. This value is unitless. Example: 100
children.label: Mass-to-charge range low value

children.name: mass_to_charge_range_high_value
children.description: The high value of the scanned mass-to-charge range, for MS1. (unitless)
children.label: Mass-to-charge range high value

children.name: mass_resolving_power
children.description: The mass resolving power, denoted as m/∆m, where ∆m is defined as the full width at half-maximum (FWHM) for a given peak with a specified mass-to-charge ratio (m/z). This measurement is unitless. Example: 60000
children.label: Mass resolving power

children.name: mass_to_charge_resolving_power
children.description: The peak mass-to-charge ratio (m/z) used to calculate the resolving power. Example: 400.2
children.label: Mass-to-charge resolving power

children.name: ion_mobility
children.description: The specific technology employed for ion mobility spectrometry. Available technologies include Traveling Wave Ion Mobility Spectrometry (TWIMS), Trapped Ion Mobility Spectrometry (TIMS), High Field Asymmetric Waveform Ion Mobility Spectrometry (FAIMS), Drift Tube Ion Mobility Spectrometry (DTIMS), Structures for Lossless Ion Manipulations (SLIM), and cyclic Ion Mobility Spectrometry (cIMS). Example: TIMS
children.label: Ion mobility

children.name: data_collection_mode
children.description: The mode of data collection used in tandem MS assays, which can include options such as Data-dependent acquisition (DDA), Data-independent acquisition (DIA), multiple reaction monitoring (SRM), or parallel reaction monitoring (PRM). Example: PRM
children.label: Data collection mode

children.name: label_name
children.description: The name or identifier of the chemical label used on this sample if labeling was applied. This is typically required for multiplexed experiments using techniques like Tandem Mass Tag (TMT). If sample was not labeled, this field may be left blank. Example: TMT126
children.label: Label name

children.name: lc_instrument_vendor
children.description: The company that manufactures the instrument used for liquid chromatography. If the instrument was custom-built or developed internally, enter "In-House". Example: Bruker
children.label: LC instrument vendor

children.name: lc_instrument_model
children.description: The model number or name of the instrument used for liquid chromatography. Example: Bruker Elute LC-MS
children.label: LC instrument model

children.name: lc_column_vendor
children.description: The manufacturer of the liquid chromatography column used, unless a self-packed or pulled tip capillary is employed. If the column was custom-made or developed internally, enter "In-House". Example: Bruker
children.label: LC column vendor

children.name: lc_column_model
children.description: The model number or name of the liquid chromatography column used. If a custom self-packed, pulled tip capillary is utilized, enter "Pulled tip capillary". Example: Thermo Scientific Vanquish UHPLC
children.label: LC column model

children.name: lc_resin
children.description: The details of the resin used in liquid chromatography, including information about the vendor, particle size, and pore size. Example: Thermo Fisher Scientific, Acclaim PepMap 100 C18, 3 µm, 100 Å
children.label: LC resin

children.name: lc_column_length_value
children.description: Liquid chromatography column length.
children.label: LC column length value

children.name: lc_column_length_unit
children.description: Units for liquid chromatography column length (typically cm).
children.label: LC column length unit

children.name: lc_temperature_value
children.description: The temperature at which the liquid chromatography (LC) process is conducted. Example: 40
children.label: LC temperature value

children.name: lc_inner_diameter_value
children.description: Liquid chromatography column inner diameter.
children.label: LC inner diameter value

children.name: lc_inner_diameter_unit
children.description: The unit of measurment for the LC inner diameter value. If the diameter is not specified, this field may be left blank. Example: um
children.label: LC inner diameter unit

children.name: lc_flow_rate_value
children.description: Value of flow rate.
children.label: LC flow rate value

children.name: lc_flow_rate_unit
children.description: Units of flow rate.
children.label: LC flow rate unit

children.name: lc_gradient_value
children.description: The liquid chromatography (LC) gradient used in the assay. Example: 120
children.label: LC gradient value

children.name: lc_gradient_unit
children.description: Unit for liquid chromatography gradient
children.label: LC gradient unit

children.name: lc_mobile_phase_a
children.description: Composition of mobile phase A.
children.label: LC mobile phase A

children.name: spatial_sampling_target
children.description: The cell type or functional tissue unit (FTU) that is the focus of the spatial profiling experiment. If the data are generated in imaging mode without targeting a specific structure, this field may be left blank. Example: Proximal tubule epithelial cell
children.label: Spatial sampling target

children.name: spatial_sampling_type
children.description: The type of spatial targeting used in the analysis. Spatial profiling focuses on selected tissue regions without necessarily producing images, while spatial imaging captures data across a regular grid of pixels, enabling visualization as ion intensity heat maps—also referred to as molecular images. Leave this field blank if the data originate from bulk (non-spatial) analysis. Example: Imaging
children.label: Spatial sampling type

children.name: analysis_protocol_doi
children.description: A DOI to a protocols.io protocol describing the software and database(s) used to process the raw data. Example: https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bsu5ney6
children.label: Analysis protocol DOI

children.name: metadata_schema_id
children.description: The unique string identifier for the metadata specification version, which is easily interpretable by computers for purposes of data validation and processing. Example: 22bc762a-5020-419d-b170-24253ed9e8d9
children.label: Metadata schema ID
